title: Limonium maurocordatae
taxonomy: Limonium maurocordatae is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Limonium, which includes over 100 species of coastal plants. The family Plumbaginaceae is a group of plants commonly known as the leadworts or plumbagos. These plants are typically found in coastal and salt-marsh environments, where they are adapted to tolerate high levels of salt. Limonium maurocordatae is native to the Mediterranean region.

intro: Limonium maurocordatae, also known as Mauro's sea lavender, is a perennial coastal plant species native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality due to its calming effects. The bioactive constituents of Limonium maurocordatae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium maurocordatae include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat insomnia, restlessness, and other sleep-related disorders.
